name: Arcos de Jalón por la Cuerda de Villalpardo — circular desde Santa María de Huerta
region_intro: Road cycling around Santa María De Huerta features routes through varied terrain, characterized by river valleys, rolling uplands, and rural landscapes. The region offers a mix of moderate and challenging ascents, often passing through areas like the Tierras de Medinaceli. Cyclists can expect routes that navigate both open countryside and more secluded areas, providing diverse riding experiences. Best road cycling routes around Santa María De Huerta The most popular road cycling route is Gargantas Río Jalón — circular por las Tierras de Medinaceli, a 43.0 miles (69.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the river gorges and rural lands of Medinaceli. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Arcos de Jalón por la Cuerda de Villalpardo — circular desde Santa María de Huerta, a moderate 44.3 miles (71.4 km) path. This route offers views from the Cuerda de Villalpardo, providing a scenic and engaging ride. Local road cyclists also love the Gorges of the Jalón River – Medinaceli loop from Iruecha, a 59.0 miles (94.9 km) trail leading through the Gorges of the Jalón River, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Road cycling around Santa María De Huerta is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and rural villages. The network offers options for both moderate and more challenging ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa María De Huerta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Santa María De Huerta?
A: The region offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, characterized by river valleys, rolling uplands, and rural landscapes. You'll find a mix of moderate and challenging ascents, often passing through areas like the Tierras de Medinaceli, providing varied riding experiences.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Santa María De Huerta?
A: While the area is known for its moderate to difficult terrain, there are no routes specifically categorized as 'easy' in this guide. Most routes involve some climbing, making them more suitable for cyclists with a bit of experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077468616/gargantas-rio-jalon-circular-por-las-tierras-de-medinaceli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gargantas Río Jalón — circular por las Tierras de Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that explores river gorges and rural lands.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Jalón River – Medinaceli loop from Iruecha&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 94.9 km trail with over 1100 meters of elevation gain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31460758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maranchón Juniper Forest – Maranchón loop from Codes&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 84 km with nearly 1200 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Santa María De Huerta are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077407144/arcos-de-jalon-por-la-cuerda-de-villalpardo-circular-desde-santa-maria-de-huerta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos de Jalón por la Cuerda de Villalpardo — circular desde Santa María de Huerta&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 71.4 km loop offering scenic views. Another popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077468616/gargantas-rio-jalon-circular-por-las-tierras-de-medinaceli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gargantas Río Jalón — circular por las Tierras de Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes around Santa María De Huerta?
A: The routes often pass through scenic rural landscapes and offer glimpses of historical sites. You might encounter attractions such as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2529637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santa María de Huerta&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montuenga de Soria Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcolea del Pinar – Anguita Village and Church loop from Codes&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you past charming villages and churches.

Q: Are there any castles or historical landmarks visible from the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Cyclists can spot several castles and historical sites. Notable examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montuenga de Soria Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Funes&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also follow parts of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5390226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santa María De Huerta?
A: The best time for road cycling in Santa María De Huerta is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the midday, while winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse road conditions, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in this guide?
A: This guide features 9 distinct road cycling routes around Santa María De Huerta, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What are the typical durations for road cycling routes in this area?
A: The routes vary in length and duration. Moderate rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077468616/gargantas-rio-jalon-circular-por-las-tierras-de-medinaceli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gargantas Río Jalón — circular por las Tierras de Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 3 hours. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Jalón River – Medinaceli loop from Iruecha&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 4 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by natural attractions like lakes?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of natural features. While not directly on every route, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5999892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Judes&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural attraction in the broader area that cyclists might consider visiting as part of their trip.

Q: What is the general terrain like for road cycling around Santa María De Huerta?
A: The terrain is characterized by a mix of river valleys and rolling uplands. You can expect a good amount of climbing, with routes featuring both moderate and challenging ascents, providing a rewarding experience for road cyclists who enjoy varied topography.
tips: This municipality is located next to the Sierra de Solorio and next to the Sima de la Cañada de Torrehermosa stream; and at an altitude of almost 900 meters. Its landscape and environment is almost more typical of the neighboring Sorian or Alcarreña lands than Aragonese.
Quiet place where you can observe different types of birds, from where you can observe the silhouette of Iruecha. Magnificent views in the middle of the juniper forest

The town of Arcos de Jalón is located in the valley of the river Jalón, close to its source. The numerous rivers and streams that cross the municipality mark the natural geography, with lush vegetation that make up the fertile plains, of great scenic richness.
